name: Lamb and Tomato Bread Lachmanjan
recipeIngredient: 1 cup unbleached all-purpose flour (or bread flour)
1/4 cup finely chopped shallots or onion
1 can (14 oz) plum tomatoes, drained and chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. To prepare the dough: In a large bowl, combine honey and water. Stir in yeast and set aside for 5 minutes. Mix together unbleached flour, salt, olive oil, and 1 cup of whole wheat flour. Knead 100 times in the same direction to properly develop gluten.
2. Gradually add more flour until the dough is stiff but still able to be stirred. Turn out onto a lightly floured surface and knead until smooth and elastic (8-10 minutes). It will still feel sticky.
3. Place the dough in a large, lightly oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let rise until doubled in volume (1 1/2 - 2 hours).
4. To prepare the topping: Heat oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add shallots and garlic, cook stirring occasionally until soft (2-3 minutes). Add lamb, cooking until it changes color (2-3 minutes). Stir in tomatoes, simmering until slightly thickened (3-4 minutes); excess liquid should be drained.
5. Stir in cinnamon, allspice, salt, and pepper. Transfer to a bowl, cover, and refrigerate until baking time.
6. To assemble the breads: Punch down the dough and turn onto a lightly floured surface. Cut into 12 pieces, cover, and let rest for 5-10 minutes.
7. Preheat oven to 450°F (230°C). Lightly oil or spray two baking sheets.
8. With a fork, place one piece of dough on each sheet. Roll out with hands or rolling pin until about a 4-inch circle. Transfer to prepared baking sheets.
9. In the center of each circle, spread about 1 1/2 tablespoons of topping, almost to the edge, and sprinkle with pine nuts. Bake in the lower third of the oven until lightly browned (7-12 minutes). Serve hot.
